executiveSummary.text: United Airlines is a high-quality global network carrier with a compelling international growth trajectory and a P/E under 10x that significantly undervalues its earnings power and asset base, and is rated HOLD.

businessModel.text:
United is one of the world's largest airlines, operating a comprehensive global network from its hubs at major US cities. Following the 2022 announcement of "United Next", the carrier has committed to a significant fleet transformation aimed at improving unit economics and competitive positioning:
    
      Mainline Passenger Revenue~85% of revenue; domestic and international scheduled service
      Cargo~5% of revenue; belly cargo on passenger aircraft and freighter operations
      Mileage Plus (Loyalty)~7% of revenue; co-brand credit card partnership and retail monetisation
       ancillary / Other~3%; ground handling, maintenance services
    
    United's "United Next" plan involves retiring older narrowbody aircraft and replacing them with a large order of Boeing 737 MAX 10s and Airbus A321neos, which offer significantly better fuel efficiency and passenger experience. The international network -- particularly the Pacific and Atlantic routes -- is United's key differentiator versus low-cost carriers and a major driver of premium revenue.

thesisEvaluation.text:
ScenarioScoreProbabilityCommentary
    
      Bull8430%International premium demand sustains; United Next fleet transition executes; Mileage Plus monetisation accelerates; EPS reaches $14-16; shares re-rate to 12-14x, reaching $140-160.
    
    
      Base7450%Steady demand environment; domestic yields stable; international growth offsets domestic competition; EPS grows to $11-13; shares reach $105-115 on 9-10x.
    
    
      Bear5820%Recession hits corporate travel; MAX 10 delays compound capacity constraints; fuel cost spike; EPS falls to $6-7; shares test $50-58.
    
  
  
    Conviction Score: 84x0.30 + 74x0.50 + 58x0.20 = 75.2
    At under 10x earnings, the margin of safety is substantial; even the base case offers strong upside and the bear case is partially cushioned by the loyalty programme valuation.

bear.scenario: bear case is partially cushioned by the loyalty programme valuation.

recommendation.text:
Rating: HOLD
    United Airlines presents one of the most compelling risk/reward opportunities in the airline sector at current levels. The shares trade at under 10x trailing earnings, approximately 19% below their 52-week high, with improving fundamentals and a valuable Mileage Plus loyalty programme that provides meaningful embedded value. The Boeing delivery risk is a legitimate concern but is already partially discounted in the share price. Target $115-125 on a 12-month horizon.
    
      FrameworkDetail
      Entry point$96.40 (current); attractive entry at under 10x earnings with meaningful downside support
      Target price (12 months)$115-$125 based on 10-11x base case EPS of $11.00-$12.00
      Stop-loss$58.00 (approximately 40% decline; below the 52-week low and near the book value floor)
      Position sizingDeep-value cyclical position; 1.5-2% portfolio weight; do not overload given fleet execution and fuel price risks
      Key trigger to upgradeQ1 2026 earnings above $2.60 per share; clarity on Boeing MAX 10 delivery timeline; transatlantic summer yields ahead of expectations

entryExit.text:
United Airlines Holdings Inc (UAL) (UAL) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $88.69–$98.33 — current levels are fairly valued. No compelling reason to add or trim here.
Add / Initiate below $88.69 — material pullback that improves risk/reward.
Trim / Take Profits above $98.33 — approaching the 52-week high at $119.21. Risk/reward no longer favourable for new capital.
Stop-loss consideration: A sustained close below $61.97 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.
